must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume lists Git, GitHub, IBM RTC, and ClearCase as version control tools used across multiple projects.
Git and GitHub are explicitly listed under 'Version Control & CI/CD' and are part of the candidate's active toolchain.
Git usage is confirmed but no explicit mention of defining branching strategies or owning repository governance.
C and Embedded C are listed as primary programming languages with 4+ years of hands-on project application.
Candidate performed statement, branch, and MC/DC code coverage analysis using VectorCAST — textbook whitebox testing.
Jenkins is listed under CI/CD tools and candidate explicitly references delivering firmware in CI/CD environments in the summary.
Candidate worked in Agile Scrum teams across multiple projects with cross-functional embedded software roles.
No explicit evidence of communication deliverables such as stakeholder presentations or documentation leadership, though defect logs and reports are mentioned.

reasoning: Shortlist with one verification gap. This candidate brings 4 years of solid automotive embedded software experience that directly overlaps with RADAR integration work — strong Embedded C coding, whitebox testing via VectorCAST with MC/DC coverage, Git-based version control, and Jenkins CI/CD exposure. The ADAS and safety-critical background (MISRA C, ISO 26262, unit testing) is highly relevant for a RADAR SW Integrator role. The key gap is branching strategy ownership — the resume shows Git usage but does not evidence the candidate has ever defined or governed a branching model, which is a named requirement for this role. Location is also a flag: this candidate is based in Bangalore, not Chennai, and the role is in-office in Chennai. Verify relocation intent before proceeding. If the candidate is open to relocating to Chennai, shortlist immediately for a technical screen.
strengths: WHITEBOX TESTING DEPTH: Designed and executed VectorCAST test harnesses with full MC/DC, statement, and branch coverage analysis on safety-critical ADAS firmware — exactly the validation depth a RADAR SW Integrator must own.

EMBEDDED C + CI/CD IN AUTOMOTIVE: Four years of active C/Embedded C development delivered inside Jenkins CI/CD pipelines on MISRA-compliant, ISO 26262-aligned codebases — rare combination at this experience level.

MULTI-TOOL VERSION CONTROL: Hands-on with Git, GitHub, IBM RTC, and ClearCase across two live automotive projects, signalling adaptability to enterprise SCM environments common in RADAR-focused OEM/Tier-1 setups.
gaps: BRANCHING STRATEGY OWNERSHIP NOT EVIDENCED: The JD explicitly requires the ability to define branching strategies, but the resume only confirms Git usage as a contributor — no evidence of repository governance, release branch management, or SCM process ownership, which is a critical gap for a Senior SW Integrator.

LOCATION MISMATCH — BANGALORE vs CHENNAI: The role is in-office in Chennai; the candidate is currently based in Bangalore. Unless relocation is confirmed, this is an operational blocker that must be resolved before screening resources are invested.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period specified in the resume and no JD maximum stated — no penalty applied.
JD requires in-office presence in Chennai; candidate is based in Bangalore — location mismatch penalty applied.
Candidate has 4+ years of experience, meeting the JD requirement of 4 years relevant experience — no penalty.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applicable.
No college exclusion or preference specified in the JD — no adjustment applied.

hard_filter_failures: Location mismatch — candidate is in Bangalore, role is in-office in Chennai
availability_reason: Latest role is listed as ending April 2026, suggesting the candidate has recently exited or is exiting employment.
rare_assets: MC/DC code coverage analysis on ADAS safety-critical systems — a niche skill typically required only in ISO 26262 ASIL-C/D contexts.
Hands-on experience with Lauterbach Trace32 and Renesas E1 debuggers — specialised automotive hardware debugging tools rarely seen at the 4-year experience level.
self_reported_unverified: Claims ISO 26262 functional safety support but provides no evidence of specific ASIL level involvement, safety case contributions, or functional safety role title.
States experience in CI/CD environments in the summary but Jenkins is only listed as a tool — no evidence of pipeline creation, maintenance, or integration ownership.
interview_flags: Ask specifically: have you ever defined or documented a Git branching strategy (e.g. GitFlow, trunk-based development) for a team — or have you only followed strategies set by others?
Probe actual Jenkins usage: did you write Jenkinsfiles, configure pipelines, or only trigger pre-built CI jobs?
Clarify relocation intent: the role is in-office in Chennai — are you open to relocating and what is your timeline?
Verify ISO 26262 depth: what ASIL level did you work under, and what specific safety work products did you author or review?

The resume shows 4 years at a single company (Mar 2022 – Apr 2026) with no earlier roles — confirm this is the candidate's only professional experience and probe the scope of independence vs. guided work.
